page: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is an x86_64 Cloud GPU instance featuring an Intel CPU with 8 shared vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 1.0 thread per core. The system is configured with 40.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 5.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, allocated at 0.3333 of a full GPU, which delivers 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 740 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The shared CPU allocation and fractional GPU configuration present a specific resource-density tradeoff. By partitioning the NVIDIA A40 GPU and utilizing shared Intel CPU resources, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated full-card deployment.

This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as moderate machine learning inference, data science tasks, small-scale model training, and graphics rendering pipelines that fit within a 16 GB VRAM envelope.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is an x86_64 Cloud GPU instance configured with 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 40.0 GB of system memory. The instance features a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, representing a 0.3333 slice of the physical GPU, which provides 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Storage is supported by a local 740 GB NVMe SSD, and one complimentary public IPv4 address is included. The shared CPU allocation and fractional GPU design offer a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for workloads that do not require a full dedicated GPU. This resource profile is suitable for moderate machine learning inference, data science processing, and graphics rendering workloads.
